after_each_use: Wipe grille and body with a dry lint-free cloth
Check XLR connector pins for bent or damaged contacts
Inspect cable strain relief for cracking
Store in protective pouch or case
monthly: Remove grille (unscrew) and clean with mild soap and water — let dry completely before reattaching
Inspect internal cartridge mount for looseness
Check for rattling by gently shaking — may indicate loose capsule
quarterly: Test frequency response with a reference source (pink noise + analyzer)
Compare output level against a known-good SM57
Inspect transformer for signs of corrosion or damage
replace_when: Rattling capsule that persists after reseating
Intermittent signal or crackling not caused by cable
Significant drop in output level (>6dB compared to reference)
Physical deformation of grille or body affecting capsule alignment

after_each_use: Wipe grille with antibacterial wipe (vocal mic — multiple users)
Check XLR connector for bent pins
Inspect windscreen for dents or deformation
Store in clip and protective pouch
monthly: Remove grille and soak in warm soapy water for 5 minutes, air dry completely
Inspect neodymium capsule — do NOT touch diaphragm
Test supercardioid pattern — check for rear rejection degradation
quarterly: Full frequency response comparison test vs reference unit
Check handling noise isolation — tap test shouldn't produce excessive thump
Inspect internal shock mount elastomers for wear
replace_when: Consistent sibilance issues not present in new units
Loss of rear rejection (supercardioid pattern degraded)
Capsule rattle or mechanical noise during handling
XLR connector loose or intermittent after resoldering

after_each_use: Wipe body clean — kick drum mics collect significant dust
Check swivel mount tightness
Inspect XLR connection
monthly: Clean grille screen with compressed air to remove dust buildup
Check mounting clip/adapter for cracks
quarterly: Compare low-frequency response (40-100Hz) against reference
Inspect internal damping for deterioration
replace_when: Loss of low-frequency extension (kick sounds thin)
Physical damage to body from drum hardware contact

after_each_use: Wipe down body and rim mount clamp
Check rim mount tension — should hold securely without over-tightening
Inspect XLR connector
monthly: Clean grille with compressed air
Inspect rim clamp rubber pads for wear — replace if torn
quarterly: Test frequency response for consistency
Check swivel joint tightness
replace_when: Rim clamp mechanism stripped or broken

after_each_use: Wipe grille and body clean
Check XLR connector pins
Store in protective case
monthly: Clean grille — remove and wash if heavily soiled
Inspect for physical damage from amp placement
quarterly: Compare output level against reference SM57/PGA57

after_each_use: Cap the microphone with protective cover
Avoid moisture — condensers are sensitive to humidity
Store with silica gel packets
monthly: Inspect diaphragm through grille for contamination (use flashlight)
Test phantom power draw — should be consistent
quarterly: Full frequency response test — condensers are more sensitive to degradation
Check for increased self-noise (hiss)

after_each_use: Wipe clean with dry cloth
Check VLM capsule alignment (look through grille)
Inspect XLR
monthly: Remove grille, clean with mild soap and water
Inspect capsule for debris
quarterly: Compare frequency response against reference

after_each_use: Wipe clean — kick mics collect heavy dust
Check stand adapter/clip
Inspect XLR
monthly: Clean grille with compressed air
Check internal VLM capsule for debris
quarterly: Test low-frequency extension and compare to reference

after_each_use: Wipe clean with dry cloth
Check rim clamp mechanism — ensure spring tension is correct
Inspect integrated cable if applicable
monthly: Clean grille screen
Check rim clamp rubber pads for wear
Inspect hinge/pivot points
quarterly: Test frequency response
Check clamp spring tension — replace if weak

after_each_use: Wipe clean — collect dust from guitar amp grilles
Check 3-position presence switch for proper function
Coil cable neatly to avoid strain
monthly: Test all 3 presence switch positions for proper operation
Clean body and grille
quarterly: Compare frequency response in all switch positions

after_each_use: Power down properly using the rear switch (not just unplugging)
Clean faders with compressed air — prevent dust from entering fader tracks
Wipe touchscreen with microfiber cloth (no liquids)
Check all I/O connections are unplugged to prevent connector strain
Cover with dust cover for transport
monthly: Run fader calibration routine (Utility > Calibrate Faders)
Update firmware if new version available (allen-heath.com/resources)
Check fan vents for dust buildup — clean with compressed air
Inspect all XLR and TRS jacks for bent pins or debris
Test all preamps with phantom power on/off for noise
Back up all show files to USB
quarterly: Full I/O test — verify every input and output channel
Check SLink / AES connections with stagebox
Inspect power cable and IEC connector for wear
Test USB recording functionality
Clean expansion card contacts if using optional I/O cards
replace_when: Faders not responding to calibration (motorized faders worn)
Preamps producing excessive noise or crosstalk
Digital artifacts or DSP glitches not resolved by firmware update

after_each_use: Clean jog wheels with microfiber cloth (no alcohol — damages coating)
Wipe faders and knobs — prevent liquid/residue from entering
Eject USB drives properly before powering down
Check all RCA and XLR outputs are disconnected
Store in padded case with dust cover
monthly: Clean channel faders with Caig DeoxIT Fader spray (remove knob caps first)
Check jog wheel sensitivity and calibration
Update firmware via Pioneer DJ website
Inspect USB ports for debris or loose connections
Test all effects and beat FX for proper operation
Check headphone jack for intermittent contact
quarterly: Full audio path test — all 4 channels, both master outputs
Test booth output independently
Check crossfader cut-in adjustment
Inspect power supply for proper voltage
Clean air vents and fan intake
replace_when: Channel faders bleed audio when fully down
Display screen failure or persistent artifacts
USB ports not recognizing drives after cleaning

after_each_use: Wipe enclosure clean with damp cloth
Check all Speakon/XLR connections
Inspect grille for dents or damage
Check mounting hardware and rigging points
monthly: Inspect speaker cones through grille for tears or deformation
Check power cable and IEC inlet
Test DSP presets and onboard processing
Verify RDNet control connectivity (if applicable)
quarterly: Full SPL output test at rated distance
Check amplifier thermal protection — run at moderate level for 30min
Inspect suspension/rigging hardware for fatigue
Update firmware if available
replace_when: Amp module producing distortion at normal levels
Rigging points showing signs of fatigue or cracking

after_each_use: Check grilles for damage — dual 18" drivers are expensive to replace
Inspect Speakon connectors
Verify casters/wheels are secure (if bottom-mounted)
Check handles for cracks or looseness
monthly: Inspect dual 18" woofer cones for tears, creasing, or delamination
Clean air vents on amplifier module
Test Crown amplifier module — listen for distortion at moderate levels
Check crossover and DSP preset operation via LCD panel
quarterly: Full power test at rated output (check for thermal limiting)
Inspect voice coil alignment (gentle push test on cones)
Check cabinet joints for separation or rattling
Verify all suspension points and M10 rigging inserts
replace_when: Woofer reconing required (significant voice coil rub)
Amplifier module thermal shutdown at normal operating levels
Driver delamination or foam surround deterioration

after_each_use: Inspect dual 18" cones through grille
Check all connections (Speakon, powerCON)
Verify caster wheel locks
Wipe exterior clean
monthly: Inspect cone surrounds for foam deterioration
Clean amplifier ventilation ports
Test DSP and limiter operation
Check internal wiring connections (remove rear panel if accessible)
quarterly: Full power output test with measurement mic
Check for rattling at high SPL (cabinet hardware, grille)
Verify polarity of all drivers
replace_when: Voice coil rub (scraping sound on excursion)

after_each_use: Power down and disconnect all cables
Wipe exterior with dry cloth
Check pole mount socket for wear
Store upright to prevent driver sag
monthly: Inspect 15" woofer and 1" HF driver through grille
Clean air vents on class-D amplifier
Test combo XLR/TRS inputs for proper signal
Check D-CONTOUR and HPF switch operation
quarterly: Full frequency response test (60Hz-20kHz coverage)
Inspect HF compression driver diaphragm
Check cabinet mounting points
replace_when: HF driver diaphragm failure (harsh distortion on highs)
Persistent buzz or rattle in cabinet

after_each_use: Disconnect column sections carefully — align pins before separating
Wipe column array elements clean
Check subwoofer connection plate for debris
Store column sections in padded bag
Inspect column pin connectors for bending
monthly: Clean all 8 column array drivers (3.5" elements)
Inspect sub 12" driver through grille
Test QuickSmart DSP presets and Bluetooth connectivity
Check power cable and connector
Verify column lock mechanism engages fully
quarterly: Full system test — column + sub as matched system
Check column element alignment and phasing
Firmware update via EV QuickSmart Mobile app
Inspect sub amp module cooling
replace_when: Column element driver failure (reduced coverage pattern)

after_each_use: Remove batteries from handheld transmitter after each event
Wipe handheld grille with antibacterial wipe
Check receiver antenna connections — hand-tighten only
Store antennas detached for transport
monthly: Clean battery contacts with isopropyl alcohol and cotton swab
Test all available frequencies for interference
Check antenna cables (BNC connectors) for damage
Scan for best available frequency group
Replace batteries preventively before events (use fresh AA)
quarterly: Full RF performance test — check range at venue distances
Compare signal quality with new BLX system (if available)
Inspect receiver rack mount hardware
replace_when: Frequent dropouts not resolved by frequency scanning

after_each_use: Remove rechargeable battery from transmitter
Charge Shure SB900A battery fully — do not leave depleted
Wipe KSM8 capsule grille with antibacterial cloth
Verify receiver powered down properly
Store antennas carefully — do not bend
monthly: Run Shure Wireless Workbench frequency coordination scan
Check SB900A battery health (cycles remaining shown on receiver)
Clean receiver ventilation
Test IR sync between transmitter and receiver
Network scan for coordinated frequencies if multi-system
quarterly: Full range test at maximum expected distance
Check KSM8 dual-diaphragm capsule alignment (Shure service if needed)
Update firmware via Shure Update Utility
Inspect rack mount ears and Ethernet port
replace_when: SB900A battery holds less than 3 hours (replace battery, not system)
Persistent RF interference not resolved by coordination

after_each_use: Allow fixture to cool before handling (lamp heat)
Return to home position (pan/tilt center) before powering down
Check safety cable attachment point
Inspect clamp tightness and truss mount
monthly: Clean optical lens and front glass with lens cloth
Clean gobo wheel — remove dust from gobo patterns
Check color wheel operation through all colors
Lubricate pan/tilt motors with white lithium grease if squeaking
Clean air filters / ventilation openings
Check DMX in/out connections
quarterly: Full DMX channel test — verify all functions respond correctly
Check lamp hours (replace lamp per manufacturer schedule)
Inspect stepper motors for smooth operation
Check internal wiring for heat damage
Test prism rotation
replace_when: Pan/tilt stepper motors failing (jerky movement, lost steps)
Color wheel or gobo wheel stuck
Excessive fan noise indicating bearing failure

after_each_use: Wipe lens clean of dust and fingerprints
Check power cable connection
Inspect housing for physical damage
monthly: Clean COB LED lens thoroughly
Test all DMX modes and color mixing
Check mounting bracket tightness
quarterly: Full color accuracy test — compare against reference
Check LED output for dimming (LED degradation)
Inspect power supply

after_each_use: Fully charge battery after each use — do not store depleted
Wipe lens and housing clean
Check wireless DMX (WiFLY) connectivity
monthly: Check battery charge cycle count — lithium batteries have limited cycles
Clean WiFLY antenna area
Test all RGBAW+UV color channels
Inspect charging port for damage
quarterly: Full battery health check — charge to 100%, measure runtime
Compare brightness output against new unit
Update fixture firmware if available
replace_when: Battery runtime drops below 50% of rated time
WiFLY wireless DMX consistently drops connection

after_each_use: Ensure all paired fixtures are unpaired/reset if no longer needed
Store in dry location — wireless electronics are moisture-sensitive
monthly: Test wireless range and signal stability at maximum expected distance
Charge/replace battery
Verify clean pairing with all fixtures
quarterly: Full range test in event-like RF environment
Check for firmware updates
replace_when: Consistent wireless dropouts at normal distances
Unit fails to pair with compatible fixtures

after_each_use: Power down properly
Disconnect DMX cables gently
Store in protective case
monthly: Test WiFi connectivity and app pairing
Verify DMX output on all 512 channels
Check firmware version
quarterly: Full network stability test over extended period
Compare DMX output accuracy with hardware controller
replace_when: WiFi module fails to connect consistently
DMX output glitching or dropping channels

after_each_use: Allow to cool before storing
Check safety cable and clamp
Clean lens of dust/haze residue
monthly: Clean optical system with lens cloth
Check zoom motor operation through full range
Test pan/tilt movement for smoothness
Clean ventilation openings
quarterly: Full DMX function test
Check LED output consistency across all 4 RGBW zones
Inspect stepper motor operation

after_each_use: Run the fog machine dry for 30 seconds after last burst (prevents clogging)
Empty remaining fluid from tank if storing for >1 week
Wipe nozzle clean of residue
Check LED array above nozzle for fog residue
monthly: Run vinegar/water flush through pump to prevent mineral buildup
Clean LED lenses above fog nozzle
Check fluid pump for proper flow rate
Inspect heater element power cable
Test DMX control of fog output level
quarterly: Full pump flush with manufacturer cleaning fluid
Check heater core element — should reach operating temp in <5 minutes
Test all LED colors while fogging
Inspect fluid tank for mold or contamination
replace_when: Heater element takes >10 minutes to heat (element degradation)
Pump cannot produce fog even with fresh fluid and clean nozzle

after_each_use: Allow lamp to cool for at least 10 minutes before moving
Return to home position before power off
Check safety cable and clamp
Inspect lens for cracks (7R lamps run very hot)
monthly: Clean front lens and internal optics with lens cleaning solution
Clean gobo and color wheels
Check lamp hours — 7R lamps last ~2000 hours
Clean fans and ventilation (critical for lamp cooling)
Lubricate pan/tilt bearings if noisy
quarterly: Full DMX function test — all channels
Check lamp alignment in reflector
Inspect internal wiring for heat damage
Replace air filters
replace_when: 7R lamp past rated hours (even if still functioning — explosion risk)

after_each_use: Check all 1/4" and XLR connections for debris
Verify ground lift switches are in default position
Store in padded case — Jensen transformers are precision components
monthly: Test both channels with a known signal source
Check pad switch operation (-15dB)
Verify ground lift switch eliminates hum when engaged
quarterly: Full frequency response test through both channels
Compare output level between channels (should match within 1dB)
replace_when: Jensen transformer failure (extremely rare — >20 year lifespan)
Connector jacks loose or intermittent after resoldering
